steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: P6 gave equal ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. But the model's predictions correlated differently with those ratings: 0.42 for abstract vs. 0.27 for cubist. The question asks what this difference in correlation implies about the model's predictions.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'model predicted P6 would rate abstract higher than cubist': a higher correlation doesn't tell us the direction of the predictions, only their accuracy relative to P6's actual ratings. ✗
  • Choice B — 'model predicted equal ratings for both styles': if predictions were equal for both, the correlations would be equally strong — contradicting the 0.42 vs. 0.27 gap. ✗
  • Choice C — 'model predicted P6 would rate the two styles differently': the unequal correlations mean the model's predictions diverged between styles, so it must have assigned different predicted ratings to abstract vs. cubist. ✓
  • Choice D — 'model predicted P6 would rate cubist higher than abstract': again, the correlation values don't determine direction — only that the model differentiated the two. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
